factors.summary: Demand trends have decelerated due to the economic impact of the pandemic, particularly affecting the company's Resources and Transportation segments with decreased revenue and canceled events.

evidence.text: we experienced a decrease to our revenue in the second and third quarters of 2020, particularly in our Resources and Transportation segments. For example, we cancelled our large customer events scheduled for the second quarter of 2020, offered voluntary price relief for dealer customers in our Transportation segment, and experienced a pause in new sales activity and cancellations from financially distressed customers, and experienced continued impacts into the third quarter.
evidence.subsection: The COVID-19 pandemic has had and could continue to have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, or cash flows.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand has significantly decreased in key segments due to the pandemic, leading to revenue declines and event cancellations.

factors.summary: Order backlog or book-to-bill has been impacted, with new orders likely lagging due to reduced demand and economic uncertainty.

evidence.text: experienced a pause in new sales activity and cancellations from financially distressed customers, and experienced continued impacts into the third quarter.
evidence.subsection: The COVID-19 pandemic has had and could continue to have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, or cash flows.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: New orders are likely lagging due to reduced demand and economic uncertainty, impacting future revenue.

factors.summary: The Financial Services end market is growing, while Transportation and Resources are facing challenges due to the pandemic and economic conditions.

evidence.text: Financial Services segment Adjusted EBITDA and associated margin continued to increase because of organic revenue growth and favorable product mix. We expect some moderation in Financial Services margin due to increased investment and an anticipated increase in lower margin services revenue. The increase in third quarter 2020 Adjusted EBITDA for the Transportation segment was due to the revenue recovery we began experiencing in the third quarter of 2020, but is still lower on a year-to-date 2020 basis due to the significant slowdown in second quarter 2020 revenue activity as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic, as well as the first quarter 2020 divestiture of the A&D business line. Resources Adjusted EBITDA and associated margin decreased due to the slowdown in organic revenue growth as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While Financial Services is growing, other segments like Transportation and Resources are facing challenges, resulting in mixed signals.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with some increases due to higher share price and tax impacts, but overall under control.

evidence.text: Within our cost of revenue and SG&A expense, stock-based compensation expense increased by approximately $2 million and $43 million for the three and nine months ended August 31, 2020, respectively, compared to the three and nine months ended August 31, 2019, which was largely due to our higher share price, related employer tax impacts associated with the exercise of stock options, and accelerations for employees impacted by cost reduction activities.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are manageable with some increases, but overall under control, indicating stability.

factors.summary: Market share is stable, with strong customer relationships and recurring revenue contributing to retention.

evidence.text: Our recurring revenue is generally stable and predictable, and we have long-term relationships with many of our customers. For the nine months ended August 31, 2020, we focused our efforts on the following actions: • Increase in geographic, product, and customer penetration.  We believe there are continued opportunities to add new customers and to increase the use of our products and services by existing customers. We plan to add new customers and build our relationships with existing customers by leveraging our existing sales channels, broad product portfolio, global footprint, and industry expertise to anticipate and respond to the changing demands of our end markets.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong customer relationships and recurring revenue suggest stable market share and growth opportunities.

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages are maintained through technology, scale, and strong customer relationships, though some segments face challenges.

evidence.text: We are a world leader in critical information, analytics, and solutions for the major industries and markets that drive economies worldwide. We deliver next-generation information, analytics, and solutions to customers in business, finance, and government, improving their operational efficiency and providing deep insights that lead to well-informed, confident decisions. We have more than 50,000 business and government customers, including 80 percent of the Fortune Global 500 and the world’s leading financial institutions. Headquartered in London, we are committed to sustainable, profitable growth. To best serve our customers, we are organized into the following four industry-focused segments: • Financial Services , which includes our financial Information, Solutions, and Processing product offerings; • Transportation, which includes our Automotive and Maritime & Trade product offerings; • Resources , which includes our Upstream, Downstream, Power, Chemical, and Agricultural product offerings; • Consolidated Markets & Solutions, which includes our Product Design; Economics & Country Risk (“ECR”), and Rootmetrics product offerings. We believe that this sales and operating model helps our customers do business with us by providing a cohesive, consistent, and effective product, sales, and marketing approach by segment.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Maintaining competitive advantages through technology, scale, and customer relationships supports a strong market position.

factors.summary: The ongoing COVID-19 pandemic has created significant economic disruption, leading to reduced consumer spending and volatility in financial markets, which has negatively impacted the company's business. Additionally, the economic environment remains a headwind due to the pandemic's continued impact, potentially leading to further risks such as lower demand and financial performance issues for customers.

evidence.text: The COVID-19 pandemic and the mitigation efforts by governments to attempt to control its spread, including travel bans and restrictions, social distancing, quarantines, and business shutdowns, have caused significant economic disruption and adversely impacted the global economy, leading to reduced consumer spending and disruptions and volatility in the global financial and commodities markets.
evidence.subsection: The COVID-19 pandemic has had and could continue to have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, or cash flows.

evidence.text: Continued economic disruption or an extended economic recession may also cause lower activity levels in the end markets we service or declining financial performance of our customers, which could result in lower demand, cancellations, reductions, or delays for our products and services.
evidence.subsection: The COVID-19 pandemic has had and could continue to have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, or cash flows.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: The pandemic has caused significant economic disruption, leading to reduced consumer spending and negative impacts on the company's business.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth has shown mixed performance, with the Financial Services segment performing well while other segments, such as Resources, Transportation, and CMS, have faced challenges due to economic impacts. Overall, there is expected pressure on organic revenue growth until economic conditions improve.

evidence.text: Organic revenue growth in Financial Services for the three and nine months ended August 31, 2020, compared to the three and nine months ended August 31, 2019, was more than offset by either flat or negative organic revenue growth in the Resources, Transportation, and CMS segments, primarily due to the current economic environment impacting Resources Upstream product offerings in the second and third quarters of 2020, the cancellations of Resources and Transportation events in the second quarter of 2020, and the off-year cycle of the Boiler Pressure Vessel Code (“BPVC”) biennial release, which was last released in the third quarter of 2019 and impacts our CMS segment.
evidence.subsection: The COVID-19 pandemic has had and could continue to have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, or cash flows.

evidence.text: we may experience continued pressure on organic revenue growth over the near term until economic conditions improve.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Organic growth is mixed with positive performance in Financial Services offset by challenges in other segments, indicating overall pressure.

factors.summary: The company's revenue mix is shifting towards more stable recurring revenue streams, but this shift is accompanied by challenges, including declines in non-recurring revenue and sector-specific issues exacerbated by the COVID-19 pandemic. The recurring fixed revenue has shown growth in certain segments, though it has been partially offset by declines in others, while recurring variable revenue remains concentrated in Financial Services. Additionally, the pandemic has negatively impacted certain segments, leading to a decrease in overall revenue.

evidence.text: Recurring fixed revenue organic growth increased 2 percent and 3 percent for the three and nine months ended August 31, 2020, respectively, compared to the three and nine months ended August 31, 2019, largely due to contributions from our Financial Services and Transportation recurring offerings, partially offset by declines in our Resources recurring offerings.
evidence.subsection: The COVID-19 pandemic has had and could continue to have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, or cash flows.

evidence.text: Recurring variable revenue was composed entirely of Financial Services revenue.

The non-recurring organic revenue decline for the three and nine months ended August 31, 2020, compared to the three and nine months ended August 31, 2019, was due to continued lower OEM activity within our Transportation segment, lower energy consulting, and a difficult year-over-year comparison in Financial Services.
we experienced a decrease to our revenue in the second and third quarters of 2020, particularly in our Resources and Transportation segments.

factors.summary: Volume trends are mixed, with some recovery observed in the Transportation segment, particularly in the third quarter, while the Resources segment continues to face declines due to lower oil prices and reduced capital expenditure spending. The overall impact of the COVID-19 pandemic has led to a pause in new sales activity and cancellations, affecting both segments negatively.

evidence.text: Unit volumes or transaction counts have declined, particularly in the Resources and Transportation segments, with a pause in new sales activity and cancellations from financially distressed customers, and experienced continued impacts into the third quarter.
evidence.subsection: The COVID-19 pandemic has had and could continue to have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, or cash flows.

evidence.text: In our Transportation segment, we saw a second quarter disruption to consumer spending that impacted our automotive customers, and we voluntarily provided temporary price relief for our dealer customers in an effort to mitigate the sudden negative impact on their sales. The segment started its recovery in the third quarter, although we experienced a slower recovery in our new car business, servicing OEMs and parts manufacturers, as customers work through lower new car inventories as a result of their second quarter COVID-19 related production shutdowns. In our Resources segment, beginning in the second quarter, we experienced pressure as a result of significantly lower oil prices and the related decrease in capital expenditure spending. These impacts continued into the third quarter, and we anticipate a continuing adverse impact on our Resources revenue as our annual subscription contracts come up for renewal and our customers focused on cost management make decisions about the amount of products and services they purchase from us.

factors.summary: The company has experienced significant pressure on its pricing power, particularly in the Transportation and Resources segments. This pressure has led to voluntary price relief being offered to dealer customers in the Transportation segment, and the company has faced challenges due to lower oil prices and reduced capital expenditure in the Resources segment.

evidence.text: The company has faced pressure on pricing power, offering voluntary price relief to dealer customers in the Transportation segment.
evidence.subsection: The COVID-19 pandemic has had and could continue to have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, or cash flows.

evidence.text: In our Transportation segment, we saw a second quarter disruption to consumer spending that impacted our automotive customers, and we voluntarily provided temporary price relief for our dealer customers in an effort to mitigate the sudden negative impact on their sales.

The segment started its recovery in the third quarter, although we experienced a slower recovery in our new car business, servicing OEMs and parts manufacturers, as customers work through lower new car inventories as a result of their second quarter COVID-19 related production shutdowns.
In our Resources segment, beginning in the second quarter, we experienced pressure as a result of significantly lower oil prices and the related decrease in capital expenditure spending.

factors.summary: The company has implemented cost reduction initiatives, which have resulted in measurable savings, particularly in cost of revenue and SG&A expenses. However, these reductions have led to increased stock-based compensation expenses, potentially affecting overall cost management.

evidence.text: proactively reducing costs intended to allow us to protect against further downside revenue risk, and investing in additional initiatives to support our long-term growth, while also focusing on maintaining liquidity and capital structure flexibility.
evidence.subsection: The COVID-19 pandemic has had and could continue to have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, or cash flows.

evidence.text: The decreases in absolute total cost of revenue and SG&A expense were largely due to the execution of our cost reduction activities we put in place at the onset of the COVID-19 pandemic. Within our cost of revenue and SG&A expense, stock-based compensation expense increased by approximately $2 million and $43 million for the three and nine months ended August 31, 2020, respectively, compared to the three and nine months ended August 31, 2019, which was largely due to our higher share price, related employer tax impacts associated with the exercise of stock options, and accelerations for employees impacted by cost reduction activities.

factors.summary: The company is facing margin pressure due to reduced revenue and cost mitigation efforts, but also shows signs of stability and slight improvements in gross and operating margins driven by cost reductions and favorable product mix.

evidence.text: Margins are under pressure due to reduced revenue and increased cost mitigation efforts, which may compress margins in the near term.
evidence.subsection: The COVID-19 pandemic has had and could continue to have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, or cash flows.

evidence.text: Our Adjusted EBITDA dollar and margin performance for the three and nine months ended August 31, 2020, compared to the three and nine months ended August 31, 2019, increased primarily because of cost reduction efforts to moderate the negative impact of revenue declines.
These near-term pressures may adversely affect our ability to grow Adjusted EBITDA as quickly as anticipated in the near term.
